insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: wave period is lowering risk — longer-period swell reaches deeper and can resuspend sediment-bound bacteria; tidal range is raising risk — large tidal swings resuspend bacteria stored in sediment and wet sand; and the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low. The last lab sample (10 MPN/100mL) is now 20 days old — beaches are sampled about weekly, so a reading this stale carries little weight, and this estimate leans on current conditions.
